description: Length-matched evaluation challenge facts for LLMs.

facts.truth: models approach ceiling performance, making the benchmark uninformative
distractors: benchmarks become increasingly discriminative as models improve on them
saturation indicates the model has achieved perfect general capability
newer models are architecturally unable to reach saturation on old tests

facts.context: Goodhart's Law in LLM evaluation means:
facts.truth: optimizing for benchmark scores may not improve real-world usefulness
distractors: high benchmark scores reliably guarantee proportional output quality
benchmarks perfectly measure all aspects of model capability at once
gaming or overfitting to benchmark metrics is fundamentally impossible

facts.context: Human evaluation of LLM outputs is limited by:
facts.truth: inter-annotator disagreement, cost, and inability to verify factual claims
distractors: perfect reliability and consistency that makes it the gold standard metric
low cost at scale enabling routine deployment for all evaluation tasks
humans reliably catching all types of errors including subtle factual ones

facts.context: Using LLMs as judges (LLM-as-judge) has the problem of:
facts.truth: inheriting biases and potentially favoring outputs similar to own style
distractors: providing perfectly objective and consistent evaluations every time
showing zero correlation with human judgment across all task categories
being strictly and demonstrably superior to human evaluators in all cases

facts.context: Distinguishing memorization from generalization is hard because:
facts.truth: novel-looking outputs may still exploit training distribution patterns
distractors: memorization is easily and reliably detected by standard output analysis
all model outputs are either fully memorized or fully novel by definition
generalization ability is directly and precisely measurable in all settings

facts.context: Multiple-choice evaluation of LLMs can be gamed by:
facts.truth: exploiting answer position biases and surface-level cues in options
distractors: multiple-choice format is inherently ungameable by language model systems
answer position biases have been shown not to exist in modern LLM models
surface cues in option phrasing are always eliminated by good test design

facts.context: Capability elicitation (finding what models can do) is challenging because:
facts.truth: performance varies greatly with prompt format and evaluation methodology
distractors: model capabilities are fully invariant to how prompts are written or formatted
a single well-designed prompt is sufficient to reveal all model abilities
elicitation methodology is a solved problem requiring no further research

facts.context: Evaluating dangerous capabilities (biosecurity, cyber) faces the dilemma of:
facts.truth: thorough testing risks demonstrating harms while insufficient testing misses risks
distractors: dangerous capabilities being perfectly safe to evaluate fully without restriction
no meaningful dilemma existing in the assessment of potentially harmful abilities
harmful uses being fundamentally impossible to demonstrate through testing alone

facts.context: Distribution shift between benchmarks and real use causes:
facts.truth: benchmark performance to poorly predict deployment performance
distractors: benchmarks to perfectly predict real-world deployment use outcomes
distribution shift effects to be negligible for modern large models
real-world deployment tasks to always be easier than benchmark tests

facts.context: Evaluating long-form generation (essays, reports) is difficult because:
facts.truth: quality is multidimensional and task-dependent without clear ground truth
distractors: output length alone reliably determines the overall quality of generation
automated metrics comprehensively capture all relevant aspects of quality
clear ground truth always exists for every long-form generation evaluation

facts.context: Detecting benchmark contamination in training data is hard because:
facts.truth: paraphrased or reformatted versions evade detection by exact matching
distractors: exact string matching reliably catches all forms of data contamination
contaminated examples always have obvious detectable statistical signatures
contamination detection is a fully solved problem requiring no new methods

facts.truth: probing for abilities that may not manifest under standard protocols
distractors: standard benchmarks automatically and comprehensively capture emergence
emergent abilities always appearing spontaneously without targeted probing
smaller scale models reliably predicting exactly what abilities will emerge
